title: Archie Goodwin

{{Top|rwp}}

|name=Archie Goodwin

|death=[[March 1]], [[1998]]<ref name="SWGC2">{{CollectorCite|2|Around the Galaxy}}</ref>

'''Archie Goodwin''' (died [[March 1]], [[1998]]) was a [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] writer and editor for nearly forty years. Goodwin was one of the first to contribute to the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] and created many characters for its rich history. Among the more notable characters and concepts Goodwin created were [[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]], the [[House of Tagge/Legends|House of Tagge]] and [[The Wheel/Legends|The Wheel]].

Goodwin begin in the comics industry in the early 1960s writing stories for [[Wikipedia:Harvey Comics|Harvey Comics]] and [[Wikipedia:Warren Publishing|Warren Publishing]]. He then started writing scripts on the [[Wikipedia:Secret Agent X-9|''Secret Agent X-9'']] syndicated strip drawn by [[Al Williamson]], as well as projects at [[Marvel Comics]] and [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC Comics]]. In [[1975]] he became the editor-in-chief at Marvel, a position he held at the time that they acquired the comic-book rights to ''Star Wars''.

==Biography==
Goodwin had a long stint on [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel's ''Star Wars'' series]], taking over from original writer [[Roy Thomas]], and wrote over 30 issues, spanning three years. In addition to writing ''Star Wars'', he was the title's editor and wrote the [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] comic adaptations.

After the passing of [[Russ Manning]], and based on his work on the Marvel Comics series, Goodwin and longtime friend Al Williamson took over Manning's [[Star Wars (comic strip)|''Star Wars'' syndicated newspaper strip]]. It was on this strip that Goodwin and Williamson began story arcs bridging the gap between [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] and ''The Empire Strikes Back''.

Goodwin died on March 1, 1998 due to cancer.<ref name="SWGC2" />
